bullets.headline: EPC Opens VOP Rulebook 2.0 Consultation Through June
bullets.detail: The EPC initiated a 90-day public consultation on change requests for the Verification of Payee scheme rulebook version 2.0, running from 1 April to 30 June 2026, with the new rulebook expected by November 2026.

bullets.headline: EPC Consults on SCT Inst at Point of Interaction
bullets.detail: The EPC launched a consultation running 6 March to 10 April 2026 exploring its potential role in facilitating interoperability of SCT Inst-based payment solutions at the Point of Interaction (POI).

bullets.headline: EPC Releases 2026 Cryptography Guidelines with PQC Focus
bullets.detail: The EPC published version 16 of its Guidelines on cryptographic algorithms usage and key management on 17 March 2026, with significant updates on Post-Quantum Cryptography considerations for payment service providers.

bullets.headline: Bank of England Finalizing November 2026 CHAPS ISO Changes
bullets.detail: The Bank of England published detailed change request documentation for the November 2026 CHAPS/RTGS ISO 20022 release, with final schemas expected in April 2026; unstructured addresses will be rejected from November 2026.

bullets.headline: ECB T2 ISO 20022 Unfreeze Step 1 Set for June 2026
bullets.detail: The ECB is progressing with its ISO 20022 message unfreeze strategy, implementing the initial step with ISO Maintenance Release 2024 in June 2026, with annual real-time maintenance to begin from November 2026.

bullets.headline: EPC Opens VOP Rulebook 2.0 Public Consultation
bullets.detail: The European Payments Council launched a 90-day public consultation (April 1 - June 30, 2026) for change requests to the Verification of Payee scheme rulebook version 2.0, with publication expected by November 2026.

bullets.headline: SWIFT Warns: 65% Still Using Unstructured Addresses
bullets.detail: SWIFT issued urgent guidance that approximately 65% of payment messages still contain unstructured addresses, with under nine months until the November 2026 deadline when unstructured postal addresses will be rejected.

bullets.headline: November 2026: camt.110 Reception Becomes Mandatory
bullets.detail: Starting November 14, 2026, all financial institutions must be able to receive ISO 20022 camt.110 Enquiry & Investigation messages through Case Management, with automatic RMA bootstrap to enforce mandatory receipt status.

bullets.headline: EPC POI Interoperability Consultation Closes April 10
bullets.detail: The EPC consultation exploring its role in SCT Inst-based payment solutions at Point of Interaction closes April 10, 2026, with potential for dedicated stakeholder workshops depending on feedback received.

bullets.headline: VOP Rulebook v1.1 Updates Effective September 2026
bullets.detail: The EPC's VOP scheme rulebook version 1.1, addressing issues identified since the October 2025 launch, along with updated API specifications and Security Framework, will become effective on September 20, 2026.

bullets.headline: VOP Rulebook 2.0 Consultation Now Open
bullets.detail: The EPC initiated a public consultation on change requests for VOP scheme rulebook version 2.0, running from 1 April to 30 June 2026, as part of regular scheme change management — stakeholders should submit feedback on proposed modifications.

bullets.headline: POI Interoperability Consultation Closing 10 April
bullets.detail: The EPC's public consultation exploring its role in interoperability of SCT Inst-based payment solutions at the Point of Interaction closes on 10 April 2026, potentially shaping future mobile/retail payment standards.

bullets.headline: November 2026 Structured Address Deadline Confirmed
bullets.detail: As of 15 November 2026, unstructured address formats will no longer be permitted in EPC payment messages across SCT, SCT Inst, and SDD schemes — PSPs must migrate to hybrid or fully structured ISO 20022 address formats.

bullets.headline: VOP Scheme v1.1 Effective September 2026
bullets.detail: The EPC published updates to the VOP scheme rulebook, API specifications, and API Security Framework that will become effective on 20 September 2026, introducing technical refinements to the newly operational scheme.

bullets.headline: ECB Comprehensive Payments Strategy Published
bullets.detail: The Eurosystem published its comprehensive payments strategy confirming digital euro pilot preparations and continued focus on strengthening SEPA and European retail payment sovereignty.

bullets.headline: VOP Rulebook 2.0 Consultation Now Open Until June
bullets.detail: The EPC initiated a public consultation on change requests for the Verification of Payee (VOP) scheme rulebook version 2.0, running from 1 April to 30 June 2026, as part of the regular change management process for EPC payment-related schemes.

bullets.headline: SCT Inst Rulebook Consultation Open Until June 11
bullets.detail: The EPC is conducting a public consultation on the 2026 SCT Inst rulebook change requests including proposals to extend name field character length from 70 to 140 characters and migration to the latest ISO 20022 standard; responses due by 11 June 2026.

bullets.headline: Unstructured Address Deadline Confirmed: 15 November 2026
bullets.detail: As of 15 November 2026, unstructured address formats will no longer be permitted in EPC payment messages across all SEPA schemes, aligned with the SWIFT MX Release date; PSPs must transition to hybrid or fully structured formats.

bullets.headline: SCT Inst at POI Consultation Closed 10 April
bullets.detail: The EPC's public consultation exploring its potential role in facilitating interoperability of instant payment solutions at Point of Interaction closed on 10 April 2026, with findings expected to shape future EPC initiatives.

bullets.headline: VOP Scheme v1.1 Effective Date: 20 September 2026
bullets.detail: Version 1.1 of the VOP scheme rulebook, API specifications, and API Security Framework will become effective on 20 September 2026, bringing updates following the successful October 2025 launch of the scheme.

bullets.headline: VOP Rulebook 2.0 Consultation Now Open Until June
bullets.detail: The EPC initiated a public consultation on change requests for its Verification of Payee (VOP) scheme rulebook version 2.0 running from 1 April to 30 June 2026, with version 1.1 taking effect on 20 September 2026.

bullets.headline: November 2026 Structured Address Deadline Confirmed
bullets.detail: As of 15 November 2026, unstructured address formats will no longer be permitted in EPC payment messages across all SEPA schemes, aligned with the SWIFT Standards MX Release.

bullets.headline: SCT Inst at POI Consultation Closes 10 April
bullets.detail: The EPC's public consultation exploring its role in facilitating SEPA Instant Credit Transfer interoperability at Point of Interaction is closing on 10 April 2026.

bullets.headline: ECB Comprehensive Payments Strategy Published
bullets.detail: The Eurosystem published its comprehensive payments strategy, confirming digital euro pilot preparations with a potential issuance target of 2029, subject to legislation adoption in 2026.

bullets.headline: PSD3 Final Agreement Expected Q1-Q2 2026
bullets.detail: Final political agreement on PSD3 between EU institutions is expected in early 2026, with national transposition and PSP system updates to follow through 2026-2027.

topic: ISO 20022 payment standard EPC European Payments Council

items.title: EPC 2026 Public Consultation on SCT Inst Scheme Rulebook Change Requests
items.summary: The EPC opened a public consultation on 2026 change requests for the SCT Inst rulebook, including proposals to extend name field character length from 70 to 140 characters and to migrate EPC payment scheme rulebooks to the latest ISO 20022 version. The consultation will inform a September 2026 EPC decision on whether 2029 rulebooks migrate to a newer ISO 20022 version (potentially the 2027 ISO version) via a big-bang changeover.

items.title: Important Update About the 2025 EPC Payment Scheme Rulebooks (Version 1.1)
items.summary: The EPC released version 1.1 of all five 2025 payment scheme rulebooks, effective 5 October 2025. The key update revises the deadline for phasing out unstructured address formats to 15 November 2026 (originally 22 November 2026), aligning with the November 2026 Swift Standards MX Release.

items.title: SEPA Request-to-Pay Implementation Guidelines Version 4.0
items.summary: The EPC published Implementation Guidelines version 4.0 for the SEPA Request-to-Pay (SRTP) scheme, setting out rules for implementing the relevant ISO 20022 XML message standards. These guidelines took effect on 5 October 2025 at 03:30 CEST.

items.title: EPC Recommendation on ISO 20022 Customer Reporting of SCTs, OCT Inst and SDDs (Version 5.0)
items.summary: Version 5.0 of the EPC customer reporting recommendation was released, providing mapping of EPC payment scheme rulebook requirements to ISO 20022 reporting messages (camt.052, camt.053, camt.054). This version includes coverage for the One-Leg Out Instant Credit Transfer (OCT Inst) scheme and took effect 5 October 2025.

items.title: SEPA Instant Credit Transfer (SCT Inst) Rulebook Version 1.1 - IPR Compliance
items.summary: The 2025 SCT Inst rulebook version 1.1 is fully compliant with EU Regulation 2024/886 (Instant Payments Regulation), amending the SEPA Regulation. The rulebook is based on the 2019 message version of ISO 20022 and is effective until 21 November 2027. Key changes include mandatory reachability requirements, hybrid address format support, and millisecond timestamps for instant transfers.

items.title: 2025 SEPA Credit Transfer Inter-PSP Implementation Guidelines Version 1.0
items.summary: The EPC published Implementation Guidelines for SEPA Credit Transfer Inter-PSP ISO 20022 XML message standards, based on the 2019 ISO 20022 standard and version 1.1 of the 2025 SCT rulebook. As of 15 November 2026, unstructured address formats will no longer be permitted in EPC payment messages.

topic: ISO 20022 payment standard EPC European Payments Council

items.title: 2025 EPC Payment Scheme Rulebooks Version 1.1 - Important Update
items.summary: The EPC released version 1.1 of all five 2025 payment scheme rulebooks (SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B, OCT Inst), effective 5 October 2025. Key change: the deadline for phasing out unstructured address formats in EPC payment messages was revised from 22 November 2026 to 15 November 2026 to align with the SWIFT Standards MX Release.

items.title: SEPA Instant Credit Transfer (SCT Inst) Rulebook 2025 Version 1.1 - IPR Compliance
items.summary: Version 1.1 of the 2025 SCT Inst rulebook is fully compliant with EU Regulation 2024/886 (Instant Payments Regulation). The rulebook is currently in effect until 21 November 2027 and includes mandatory reachability requirements aligned with IPR deadlines for EEA-based participants.

items.title: SEPA Request-to-Pay Implementation Guidelines Version 4.0
items.summary: The EPC published implementation guidelines for ISO 20022 XML message standards for the SEPA Request-to-Pay (SRTP) scheme rulebook version 4.0, which took effect on 5 October 2025 at 03:30 CEST.

items.title: EPC Recommendation on ISO 20022 Customer Reporting (Version 5.0)
items.summary: Updated version 5.0 provides mapping of EPC payment scheme rulebook requirements to ISO 20022 reporting messages (camt.052, camt.053, camt.054). This version now also covers recommendations for the One-Leg Out Instant Credit Transfer (OCT Inst) scheme, effective 5 October 2025.

items.title: ECB ISO 20022 Message Unfreeze Strategy Status Update
items.summary: The ECB outlined its ISO 20022 message unfreeze strategy: implementing ISO Maintenance Release 2024 in June 2026 and annual real-time maintenance starting November 2026. TIPS core payment messages under EPC governance will remain on ISO MR2019 aligned with international market practice.

items.title: November 2026 Deadline: Mandatory Structured Address Formats in EPC Payment Messages
items.summary: As of 15 November 2026, unstructured address formats will no longer be permitted in EPC payment messages. Only fully structured or hybrid address formats will be accepted, with Town Name and Country as minimum required structured elements.
